prompt: The Fed meeting is on Wednesday at 2pm. It's currently Monday morning. The market expects a 25bps hold but there's talk of a hawkish surprise. SPY is at $505. How should I position my options for this event?

assertions.text: Identifies the FOMC timing mechanics (2pm decision, 2:30pm press conference)
Advises that IV peaks on Wednesday morning (before the decision) - good time to sell
Provides at least two distinct strategy options (e.g., directional bearish AND neutral premium-selling)
Gives specific SPY strike guidance or delta guidance for the recommended trade
Mentions sector impact (financials, rate-sensitive sectors like utilities/REITs)
Addresses exit timing (when to close relative to the Wednesday event)
